summary: Data pipeline and checkpoint management has 5 real issues and 1 fake finding.

findings.title: CPU-side image augmentation instead of GPU
evidence_refs: nsys: CPU utilization spikes during data loading, GPU idle gaps
findings.metric: ~30% GPU idle time waiting for augmented data
findings.description: ImageTransformPipeline.augment() runs all transforms (flip, crop, resize) on CPU. For large batches, this creates a CPU bottleneck. GPU-based augmentation (kornia, torchvision GPU ops) would keep data on GPU.
findings.suggestion: Move augmentation to GPU using kornia or torchvision GPU transforms, or use DALI for CPU-GPU pipelining.

findings.title: Per-sample normalization instead of batched
evidence_refs: nsys: many small element-wise ops in data pipeline
findings.description: ImageTransformPipeline.normalize() is called per-sample in __getitem__. Normalization should be applied to the entire batch at once after collation for better throughput.
findings.suggestion: Move normalization to a batch-level transform or apply in the training loop after batch collation.

findings.title: pin_memory not enabled in DataLoader
findings.description: DataLoader is created with pin_memory=False. Pinned memory enables asynchronous CPU→GPU transfers via a separate CUDA stream, overlapping data transfer with computation.

findings.title: Synchronous checkpoint save blocks training
evidence_refs: nsys: GPU idle during checkpoint save, cudaMemcpy D2H
findings.description: CheckpointManager.save() calls torch.save() synchronously, blocking the training loop while model state is serialized to disk. Should clone to CPU and save in background thread.
findings.suggestion: Clone state_dict to CPU, then save in a daemon thread to avoid blocking training.

findings.title: non_blocking=True missing on .to(device)
evidence_refs: nsys: synchronous H2D transfers in training step
findings.description: TrainingLoop.train_epoch() transfers data to GPU with .to(device) without non_blocking=True. Combined with pin_memory, non_blocking enables async transfers.

findings.title: Use channels_last memory format for convolution
evidence_refs: nsys: NCHW format visible in conv kernels
findings.description: The model uses NCHW (default) memory format. NHWC (channels_last) is often faster on CUDA with tensor cores due to better memory access patterns.
findings.suggestion: Convert model and inputs to channels_last format with .to(memory_format=torch.channels_last).
